meaning: Three special letters: Alif, Lam, Ra (that begin this chapter)
then (connects this ayah to the previous one)
and a giver of good news
he will give you all enjoyment (if you seek forgiveness)
so that they may hide themselves
All-Knowing (as an inherent quality of Allah)
and what/not - a combination of 'and' plus a word that can mean 'what' when asking questions or 'not' when negating
so that he may test you all
which of you (asking about who does better deeds)
and indeed if (connects to previous statement)
this (pointing to what the disbelievers say next)
except (negates what comes before it in this ayah)
he will most surely say indeed
turned away/diverted (describing the punishment in this ayah)
they were (referring to those who mock in this ayah)
from us (referring to the mercy being taken away)
truly one who loses hope a lot
blessing (that Allah gave him after hardship)
those (far away, referring to the patient believers mentioned before)
some/part (of what is revealed to you)
you all were (addressing multiple people in the past)
so/then are? (asking if something follows from previous statement)
those who submit completely to Allah (plural)
we fulfill completely (to them their deeds)
they work (referring to those who disbelieve in the ayah)
Then is the one who...? (asking about someone)
he was (introduces a description in this ayah)
of your Lord (who cares for and helps you grow)
these ones/these people (who are being referred to)
those (far away, pointing to the disbelievers mentioned)
away from them/about them (referring to false gods they made up)
and they became humble and submissive
their Lord (the one who nurtures and cares for them)
those (plural, far away, referring to the believers mentioned before)
the Paradise (referring to the eternal garden in the afterlife)
the two distinct groups (being compared: the blind/deaf versus the seeing/hearing)
a warner (someone whose job is to warn others)
and then he said (after what came before in the story)
we think you all (without being sure)
a mercy (that Allah gave to Nuh)
so it was made unclear (referring to the evidence/sign mentioned)
shall we compel you all to accept it?
those who (referring to the believers mentioned after)
indeed they (referring to those who believed)
And O my people! (in possessive/genitive case)
they said (the people speaking to Noah)
and not - joins two ideas and makes the second one negative
if (introduces the condition 'if I wanted' before 'my advice would not benefit you')
He (referring to Allah in this context)
or (asking a question to challenge what was said before)
and make! (command to Noah to build the ark)
under our watchful eyes (watching over the construction of the ark)
you speak to me (don't speak to me!)
you all mock (referring to multiple people)
a mountain (that he was seeking refuge on)
between them both (separating the son and his father)
Mount Judi (the elevated mountain where Noah's ark rested)
then he said (after Nuh called upon his Lord)
the people who don't know or understand
and you show mercy to me
with (a special kind of divine) peace (that comes from Allah)
that (feminine, pointing to something far away)
we reveal it (referring to these stories of the unseen)
to you (the one being told about the stories)
you know it/her (referring to the stories/news mentioned before)
Hud (as their brother who was sent to them)
except (what comes after this word is excluded from what was mentioned before)
those who make up false things
O my people! (addressing his own community)
indeed (emphasizing that the speaker's reward is only from Allah)
he created me (referring to Allah's creation)
all of you return back! (plural command)
the sky (as the object being sent)
heavily pouring (describing how the sky sends rain)
a power, a strength (as the object being added to)
those who commit wrong (being turned away from)
he said (in response to their accusation)
except (excluding what comes after from what was mentioned before)
He (referring to Allah in this ayah)
by/with its forelock (referring to every creature mentioned before)
what/that which (emphasized, referring to the message sent)
I was sent (as a messenger to you all)
a people (who will be brought as replacement)
my Lord (the one who nurtures and sustains me)
and that (feminine, pointing to something far)
and they were made to follow
their Lord (the one who nurtures and cares for them)
Now behold! (used to draw attention to what follows)
surely for Aad (emphatic, referring to the tribe's punishment)
He (referring to Allah in this ayah)
He created you all (Allah is the subject)
and He made you all build/develop (referring to Allah)
so seek forgiveness from Him! (command to many people)
hoped for (as the object being described)
from what (that you call us to)
and he gave me/brought to me
and then he will seize you all (as punishment)
and he took/seized (connects to previous ayah)
Thamud (as the object being referred to)
peace! (as a greeting to the angels who visited Ibrahim)
their hands (referring to the guests' hands that did not reach the food)
to him (reaching him in the ayah)
from them (referring to the angels who were guests)
standing (describing his wife who was standing)
so she laughed/smiled (after hearing the news)
and we gave her good news
she said (speaking to her husband about having a child)
my husband (speaking about her husband in this ayah)
and indeed they (connecting to the previous statement about Allah's command)
coming to them (referring to the punishment coming to the people being addressed)
he was troubled/made to feel bad
to him (referring back to the person being rushed to)
they work/act/do (referring to the people who were doing evil deeds)
he said (introducing what he tells his people)
so protect yourselves! (command to group in response to previous statement)
you knew/understood (addressing someone in the ayah)
if only (expressing a wish that didn't happen)
with you all (referring to the group Lut is addressing)
a power/strength (that Lut wished he had)
O Lot! (being called by the angels)
from you all (referring to Lut's family)
anyone (in the context of 'not anyone from you')
and we caused to rain down (stones)
upon it/her (referring to the city that was turned upside down)
stones (as the object being rained down)
far from (something) (in the ayah: not far from the wrongdoers)
you all reduce unfairly (command not to do this)
upon you all, over you all
we want/wish (in response to what they said about their wealth)
the one who is very patient and gentle
except/only (in this ayah: showing that reform/improvement is his only goal)
And O my people! (connecting to previous statement)
let not my disagreement cause harm to you all
and seek forgiveness, all of you
you all turn back! (command to group)
The Most Kind and Caring One
we would surely have stoned you
my Lord (the one who takes complete care of me)
with you all (addressing the people Shuaib is speaking to)
then they became/entered into a state
not (makes a present verb into past negative)
they dwelt (referring to the people of Madyan)
and an authority (as proof or evidence)
so they followed closely (referring to Pharaoh's people)
his people (referring to Pharaoh's people whom he leads)
and they were made to follow
and (some were) mowed down (referring to towns)
from/away from them (referring to their gods that they called upon besides Allah)
they call/invite (referring to their false gods in this ayah)
he took/seized (referring to how your Lord seizes cities)
a wretched one (describing some of the people on Judgment Day)
and a fortunate one (contrasting with the miserable ones)
a heavy breathing sound (of those in Hell-fire)
except/unless (excluding what comes after from what was mentioned before)
cut off/interrupted (describing something owned by or connected to something else)
their fathers (referring to the disbelievers mentioned earlier)
We will certainly give them in full
and if not for (connects to previous statement)
surely it would have been decided
between them (referring to those who disagreed about the Book)
all of them (as the object receiving complete reward)
He (your Lord) will most definitely pay them in full
All-Seeing, All-Aware (describing how Allah sees and knows everything)
then it will touch you all intensely
and not (in this ayah: and you do not have protectors besides Allah)
Allah (as the object being referred to)
so why not / then why didn't
a small amount (of those who forbade corruption)
they were made to live in luxury
and its people (referring to the towns)
those who fix/make things better (in the ayah: while they were doing good)
he created them (referring to those who will fill Hell, mentioned later in the ayah)
